title: QA: PermissionManager::extract_target hard-codes three tool names; new tools fail-open
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
decoupling
## Location
src/permissions.rs:270-302
## Violation
`extract_target` exhaustively enumerates only three permission-relevant
tools:
```rust
match tool_name {
    "bash" => { ... }
    "edit_file" => { ... }
    "write_file" => { ... }
    // Read-only tools, task tools, and memory tools don't need permission checks
    _ => None,
}
```
The `_ => None` catch-all means **any tool added elsewhere in the code
base bypasses permission checks entirely**. The list lives in
permissions.rs; the tool definitions live in tools.rs (per CLAUDE.md
architecture map). There is no compile-time link between the two. A
contributor adding `delete_file`, `chmod`, `run_subprocess`, or an MCP
tool gets no warning — just silent allow-all. This is an OCP violation
(open for modification, not extension) and a security gap.
## Mandated refactor
Invert the dependency: have each tool register its permission target via
a trait method `fn permission_target(&self, args: &Value) ->
Option<PermissionTarget>` that the `PermissionManager` queries. Or
replace the `match` with a `HashMap<&str, TargetExtractor>` populated by
a registration macro in tools.rs at compile time. Add a `#[deny]` or
build-time assertion that every tool name registered in `tools.rs`
appears in the permission table.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
